data.title: What are the main events for today?
data.description:
Today it's the Flash PMIs Day for the Eurozone, the UK and the US. These are generally market moving releases and they might influence the markets expectations for interest rates. We will also get the Canadian Retail Sales data in the American session and the final UMich Consumer Sentiment report.

Here are the forecasts:

  • France Manufacturing PMI: 45.5 expected vs. 45.0 prior.
  • France Services PMI: 48.9 expected vs. 48.2 prior.
  • Germany Manufacturing PMI: 45.5 expected vs. 45.0 prior.
  • Germany Services PMI: 52.5 expected vs. 52.5 prior.
  • Eurozone Manufacturing PMI: 47.0 expected vs. 46.6prior.
  • Eurozone Services PMI: 51.5 expected vs. 51.3prior.
  • UK Manufacturing PMI: 48.4 expected vs. 48.3prior.
  • UK Services PMI: 50.8 expected vs. 50.8 prior.
  • US Manufacturing PMI: 51.5 expected vs. 51.2prior.
  • US Services PMI: 53.0 expected vs. 52.9 prior.

Flash PMIs

Central bank speakers:

  • 14:30 GMT/09:30 ET - ECB's Lane (dove - voter)
  • 16:30 GMT/11:30 ET - Fed's Daly (neutral - non voter)
  • 16:30 GMT/11:30 ET - Fed's Jefferson (neutral - voter)
  • 17:45 GMT/12:45 ET - BoC's Macklem (neutral - voter)

data.title: Market Chatter: China's Liquidity Injection Fails to Ease Money Market Squeeze
data.description:
China's central bank ramped up liquidity injections, but borrowing costs remained high, signaling persistent tightness in the money market, Bloomberg News reported Friday.

The People's Bank of China on Friday added a net 84 billion yuan in short-term funds on Friday, its largest daily injection this month, according to the report.

Despite the move, the seven-day repo rate climbed 14 basis points to 2.2%, while the overnight rate stayed near an eight-month high, Bloomberg wrote. The 10-year bond yield also rose, reflecting continued market pressure.

The PBOC has kept liquidity tight to support the yuan, but this has strained the bond market and fueled a stock rally, according to the report.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: DJI: Dow Jones Sheds 450 Points as Walmart Outlook Dents Growth Prospects
data.description:
Key points:
  • Dow Jones tumbles 1%
  • Walmart sinks equities
  • Amazon dethrones the king

Imagine being so big, your guidance causes the nation’s stock indexes to stumble. That’s what Walmart just did.

🧐 Dow Slides 1%

  • The Dow Jones Industrial Average index washed out 450 points, or 1%, on Thursday after the heavyweight of America’s retail world dropped its earnings update. It was so earth-moving that stocks sold off immediately at the opening bell. Both Dow’s peers — S&P 500 and Nasdaq — also tanked under the flatline.
  • Walmart stock slipped 6.5%, dragging everything with it, as the retail giant issued a disappointing outlook. Walmart said it expects profits to slow down in 2025 as the “geopolitical landscape” is far from certain.

🛒 Walmart, the Main Character

  • Walmart’s guidance spooked traders as it painted a rather dire outlook for the US economy. The retailer is regarded as the bellwether for consumer behavior. In its most-recent quarter, Walmart saw its revenue slide to number two after holding the top spot for years — since 2012 to be precise.
  • Walmart’s sales amounted to $180.5 billion for the December quarter while Amazon’s revenue reached a record $187.8 billion. Walmart is still expected to beat Amazon for the fiscal year with $708.7 billion against $700.8 billion. But can it sustain its reign as the highest-revenue company?

🐦 From a Bird’s-Eye View

  • Back to stocks and indexes — the S&P 500 booked a 0.4% loss and the Nasdaq Composite wiped out 0.5%. Tech highflyers, financials and, of course, consumer stocks, were among the biggest losers for the session. Health care and real estate were the only bright spots on the day.
  • No major news is on deck for Friday, allowing traders to take a breath, reassess the situation and go hunt those profitable trading opportunities.

data.title: Lithuania Industrial Output at 6-Month High
data.description:
Industrial production in Lithuania advanced by 8.8% year-on-year in January 2025, following a 5.9% rise in the previous month.

This marked the sixth consecutive month of growth in industrial activity and the strongest since July 2024, as output accelerated for mining and quarrying (36.5% vs 0.4% in December) and manufacturing (10.4% vs 6.9%).

Meanwhile, production declined further for electricity, gas, steam and air-conditioning supply (-8.9% vs -4.3%) and water supply, sewerage, waste management and remediation services (-2.4% vs -1.1%).

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, industrial activity increased by 4.5% in January, following a 2.2% gain in the preceding period.

data.title: China says vice premier He set to have video call with US Treasury secretary Bessent
data.description: The call is said to "communicate issues in the economic field" between both countries. There's no details on when that call will take place but it is something that we might expect some headlines from either over the weekend or next week perhaps.

data.title: UK's Monthly Retail Sales Rise 1.7% in January
data.description:
Britain's monthly retail sales climbed 1.7% in January after a revised 0.6% decrease in the previous month, data from the Office for National Statistics showed Friday.

Analysts expected a 0.3% increase for the month.

On a yearly basis, UK retail sales edged up 1%, against the revised 2.8% growth earlier and the consensus estimate of a 0.6% rise.

Excluding automotive fuel, retail sales grew 2.1% month over month and 1.2% annually, following the revised 0.9% fall and revised 2.1% jump, respectively. The figures compare with the market forecasts of 0.9% monthly growth and a 0.5% yearly gain.

data.title: Copper Set to End Week Lower
data.description:
Copper futures fell below $4.60 per pound on Friday and were on track to finish the week lower, as traders continued to assess the potential impact of US President Donald Trump’s tariff threats.

Trump recently added lumber and forest products to the list of key sectors targeted for tariffs, although copper has so far been excluded.

The President also suggested that a new trade deal with China, the world’s largest copper consumer, could be possible, signaling a more positive outlook for US-China relations.

Trump described his relationship with President Xi Jinping as “great,” though he provided no specific details or timeline for a potential deal.

Meanwhile, Chinese authorities have imposed restrictions on copper smelting due to overcapacity in the industry.

This excess production has resulted in higher copper imports and declining inventories, although smelting firms are struggling to maintain profitability.

data.title: Compagnie De Saint-Gobain: Thuaudet Joined Saint-Gobain in Sept 2019
Elekta to Deliver Eight Linear Accelerators to Mexican Institute
data.description:
Elekta (EKTA-B.ST) secured an order to supply eight linear accelerators to the Instituto Mexicano del Seguro Social, or IMSS, in Mexico.

The Swedish radiation therapy company will also partner with Dutch electronics company Royal Philips for IMSS's radiation oncology therapy simulation imaging requirements, according to a Friday release.

Additionally, installation and construction-related matters for equipment will be carried out in collaboration with Mexican medical equipment supplier Human Corporis.

data.title: U.K. Retail Sales Rise for First Time in Five Months
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

Retailers in the U.K. reported a brighter start to the new year, with sales rising after four straight months of falls, largely from a recovery in the food segment.

U.K. retail sales volumes were 1.7% higher in January than in the prior month, from a fall of 0.6% in December, the Office for National Statistics said Friday. Economists expected sales to climb 0.9% on month, according to a poll by The Wall Street Journal.

Food store sales volumes jumped 5.6%, the largest rise since March 2020, following falls in recent months, the ONS said.

However, sales fell over the three months to January and remain below prepandemic levels, it added.

The uptick in January nevertheless comes after consumer confidence in the U.K. edged up this month, in data also published Friday, likely lifted on the back of the Bank of England's interest-rate cut earlier in February.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: Petronas Chemicals Shares Jump After Sharply Higher Quarterly Profit
data.description:
By Ying Xian Wong

Petronas Chemicals shares rose sharply after the Malaysian petrochemical company reported a more than fourfold increase in fourth-quarter profit.

The company's shares jumped as much as 11% in Friday afternoon trade, and were last 8.9% higher at 4.03 ringgit, trimming year-to-date losses to about 22%.

Petronas Chemicals said earlier Friday that fourth-quarter net profit jumped to 519 million ringgit, equivalent to $117.2 million, from 112 million ringgit for the same period a year ago. The profit increase was mainly due to higher sales volumes and foreign-exchange gains on the revaluation of payables for a joint operation entity.

Quarterly revenue was 3.4% higher at 7.46 billion ringgit.

Looking ahead, market conditions are expected to remain largely unchanged in 2025 amid uncertainties stemming from shifting economic policies around the world, potential retaliatory actions by affected countries and ongoing geopolitical events, Petronas Chemicals Chief Executive Mazuin Ismail said in a statement.

Meanwhile, the oversupply in global petrochemicals remains a challenge, with capacity additions expected to outpace demand growth by about 50% in 2025, he said.

Since late 2024, prices and spreads in olefins and derivatives have declined and narrowed, with signs that Southeast Asian integrated spreads may stay weak, he added.

Write to Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com

data.title: Compagnie De Saint-Gobain: CFO Sreedhar N. Will Become Asia-Pacific Region CEO
Chinese Shares End Higher Amid Positive Sentiment Toward Tech — Market Talk
data.description: Chinese shares closed higher amid positive sentiment over tech stocks and Chinese AI firm DeepSeek. Alibaba's latest earnings are buoying the upbeat mood toward Chinese tech stocks and foreign investors' interest in Chinese equities after DeepSeek's tech breakthrough. GF Securities led the gains, up 2.1%. Citic Securities and Shenzhen Inovance Technology rose 2.0% each. CATL closed 2.0% higher after signing an MOU with Volkswagen on EV battery R&D. Meanwhile, Haier Smart Home closed 0.8% lower and ENN Natural gas fell 0.8%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index ended 0.85% higher at 3379.11 and the Shenzhen Composite Index ended 1.6% higher. The ChiNext Price Index added 2.5%. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: Jiangxi Synergy Pharmaceutical Secures Drug Registration in South Korea
data.description:
Jiangxi Synergy Pharmaceutical said it received a drug registration certificate from South Korea's Ministry of Food and Drug Safety (MFDS) for its empagliflozin active pharmaceutical ingredient (API), used in treating type 2 diabetes, according to a Wednesday filing with the Shenzhen Bourse.

This approval grants the company market access in South Korea, supporting its international expansion. The certification was issued on Feb. 18.

data.title: UK Public Sector Surplus Rises
data.description:
Public sector net borrowing, excluding public sector banks, in the UK was in surplus by £15.44 billion in January 2025, up from the £14.69 billion surplus in the corresponding month last year, but below the expected £20 billion.

This marks the highest January surplus since monthly records began in 1993, as total receipts increased £8.1 billion year-over-year, driven by higher receipts from self-assessed taxes.

This month saw the highest January amount on record for combined self-assessed income and capital gains tax receipts.

data.title: The Shangai Composite Index Closes 0.86% Higher
data.description:
In China, the Shangai Composite Index rose 29 points or 0.86 percent on Friday.

Leading the gains are China United Network (10.00%), Aisino (4.27%) and Jiangsu Hengrui (3.74%).

Top losers were SAIC Motor (-2.54%), China Shenhua Engy (-2.32%) and Bank Of Beijing (-2.16%).

data.title: European Markets Set for Muted Open
data.description:
European equity markets were poised for a flat opening on Friday as investors continued to digest mixed results from the ongoing earnings season and monitor developments in global trade policies.

Traders will focus on UK retail sales and French business confidence data, as well as the latest PMI reports from across the region.

On the corporate front, earnings reports are expected from companies including Air Liquide, Standard Chartered, and Leonardo SpA. In premarket trade, Euro Stoxx 50 and Stoxx 600 futures were fluctuating near the flatline.

data.title: Evening Wrap: ASX 200 logs fifth-straight loss despite Domain takeover and solid gains in iron ore majors BHP, RIO and FMG
data.description:
The S&P/ASX 200 closed 26.6 points lower, down 0.32%.

Notable winners on the ASX today included a bouncing Mineral Resources (MIN) (+5.0%) (it copped an upgrade to "Overweight" from Barrenjoey), but the major iron ore miners flexed the most muscle, with Rio Tinto (RIO) (+2.8%), BHP Group (BHP) (+2.8%), and Fortescue (FMG) (+2.4%) each showing solid gains.

It was another "barbell" performance for Aussie stocks though, with other sectors continuing to struggle. The banks lopped off even more index points for the week as the fallout from their recent results continue to weigh on investor sentiment, and takeover news in the Communications Services sector sent the share prices of Domain Group (DHG) (+40%) and Nine Entertainment (NEC) (+20%) soaring at the expense of REA Group (REA) (-11.4%) and News Corp (NWS) (-6.5%).

Click/scroll through for the usual reporting of the major sector and stock-specific moves, the broker responses to them, as well as all the key upcoming economic data in tonight's Evening Wrap.

Also, I have detailed technical analysis on the ASX 200, NASDAQ Composite, Iron Ore and China A50 shares in today's ChartWatch.

Let's dive in!

Today in Review

Markets

ASX 200 Session Chart

The S&P/ASX 200 (XJO) finished 26.6 points lower at 8,296.2, % from its session high/low and just % from its high/low. In the broader-based S&P/ASX 300 (XKO), advancers lagged decliners by a disappointing 104 to 167.

For the week, the XJO finished down 259 points or 3.13% lower over 5 consecutive down days, 3.13% from its intraweek high and just 0.10% from its intraweek low. Yuk 🤢!

S&P/ASX 200 (XJO) weekly chart (

 for full size image)

I don't want to encroach too much on ChartWatch in this section, but for long suffering readers of this Evening Wrap, you know that long black candle and close just 0.1% from its low is not a great look! We've come back from moves like this before, though, and my experience suggests it's unwise to bet against the Bull too early. 🐂

When will it not be "too early"!? I hear you ask. Let's say I wouldn't want to see a close below my short term uptrend ribbon for starters. Then, lower peaks and troughs are usually a good confirmation we're in for more than just a garden variety pullback...but let's not get too far ahead of ourselves here – the candles and price action will do the talking – it's our job to do the listening! 👂👂

Back to the usual wrap stuff...and what can I say, it was less lousy today than the past three days but still lousy nonetheless...

Clearly the problem for the ASX 200 remains the hemorrhaging of index points from the heavily weighted Financials (XFJ) (-1.3%) sector. The Big 4 banks continued to slide today, led by Commonwealth Bank of Australia (-2.6%), but the regionals and Macquarie Group (-1.5%) were also lower. Sector losses would have been worse if not for a strong gain in QBE Insurance Group (+3.0%) after it reported well received first half results.

Consumer Discretionary (XDJ) (-1.5%), was actually the biggest losing sector on the ASX today. It's also struggled this week, partly earnings related, but I propose mostly RBA disappointment related. Fewer rate cuts – by order of magnitude 50 basis points from what the market was expecting at the start of the week – likely did the big damage here.

More specifically for the XDJ today, though, was a big drop in Guzman y Gomez (-14.2%) following the release of its first half results. Ongoing weakness in sector heavyweight Wesfarmers (-1.9%) also didn't help as brokers generally echoed their "overvalued" calls in the wake of yesterday's first half results – even as they raised their price targets on the stock (see Broker Moves section for more details).

The biggest news story of the day, however, was undoubtedly the massive pop in Domain Australia (+40.0%) after US property giant CoStar made bid for the company at a price of $4.20 per share – substantially more than yesterday's $3.12 closing price. Nine Entertainment (+20.1%) owns roughly 60% of the property platform, so naturally, they got a big boost today also.

Going the other way were the competition, namely major rival REA Group (-11.4%) and parent News Corporation (-6.5%). The idea here is that the well-backed CoStar will put up a much better fight in the space than Domain was doing. Falls in REA and NWS dragged the Communication Services (XTJ) sector to a 1.3% loss on the day.

There were winners in today's trade – yes, another barbell session! Resources (XJR) (+1.5%) enjoyed stronger base metals pricing overnight, particularly in aluminium which hit a near-2-year high, but the iron ore price was also nicely higher. I note Chinese equities continue to strengthen, likely on the back of ongoing speculation that PBOC interest rate cuts are imminent. (I have full technical analysis on iron ore and China A50 shares for you in ChartWatch below!).

Notable winners in Resources included a bouncing Mineral Resources (+5.0%) (it copped an upgrade to "Overweight" from Barrenjoey – see Broker Moves section), but the major iron ore miners flexed the most muscle, with Rio Tinto (+2.8%), BHP Group (+2.8%), and Fortescue (+2.4%) each showing solid gains.

ChartWatch

NASDAQ Composite Index

What's all the fuss about? 🤔 (click here for full size image)

The last time we covered was in ChartWatch in the Evening Wrap on 14 February.

In that update, I said:

No bull market rises in a straight line. There are the inevitable phases of short term equilibrium (similar to the current environment), and even potential short term bouts of supply-side control. It’s a dance. But, as long as that dark green ribbon is just that – dark green – and as long as the price continues to close above it, then in my experience the bull market is intact.

Ironically, the Comp has been just fine since that update, barely a wiggle in its prevailing short and long term trends – compared to us – who appear to be holding true to the “not in a straight line” adage! 📈

Nothing straight about our line…📉

S&P/ASX 200 (XJO) daily chart (click here for full size image)

Back to the Comp chart, because the above chart of the XJO is just too darn depressing! 😭

For the Comp I can still see:

✅ Price is closing above short and long term trend ribbons which are rising

✅ Short and long term trend ribbons appear to be acting as zones of dynamic demand

✅ Price action is rising peaks and rising troughs (indicating demand reinforcement and supply removal)

✅ The candles are predominantly demand-side in nature (i.e., white-bodied and or downward pointing shadows indicating accumulation and buy the dip activity)

So, all in all, it’s market that remains consistent with demand-side control.

Clearly, though, we’re working slowly through some (I suggest) substantial overhead excess supply between here and 20205. That makes sense. All-time highs – particularly those that have stood for some time – can attract to them motivated supply.

This supply must be consumed by the demand-side before we can move higher. You’ll know that’s occurred when you see a long white candle with a high close above 20205.

Until then, I’m happy to stay the course – because I can’t see anything in the chart which suggests otherwise.

But! I can’t see the next candle, nor the one after that. So, until I update the Comp in ChartWatch again, it will be your job to look for those and do the requisite analysis.

Remember here: Supply-side candles are those with black bodies and or upward pointing shadows. The bigger the body / shadow, the bigger the supply lurking in the system there. Also consider that a low close is a strong indicator of sustained excess supply right into the close.

Demand is at the short term uptrend ribbon, and below it next at 19141-19415. The short term uptrend remains intact as long as the Comp continues to close above those levels. Below them, the rising long term uptrend ribbon continues to define the path that runs between bull and bear market.

Iron Ore 62% (Front month, back-adjusted) SGX

Probably better than most expected (click here for full size image)

The last time we covered iron ore was in ChartWatch in the Evening Wrap on 13 February.

In that update, we were tracking improving Chinese stock prices (I have the chart of the China A50 below) and the accompanying improvement in the iron ore price.

It makes sense that the two should occur at the same time. Hand in glove sort of stuff.

There’s possibly also some positive ramifications for our Resources sector, but that’s a chart for another day!

For now, I note the iron ore chart appears to show growing demand-side influence. The last pullback to the short term uptrend ribbon looked well supported with two downward pointing shadows into the middle of the short term dynamic demand zone. (A great example of how downward pointing shadows and the demand zone can combine so well together!).

Thursday’s white candle with high close was particularly encouraging (Note: the last white candle on the chart is today’s candle – it is live – so discount it because it doesn’t exist to us!).

If we see a close above 108.80 it would like see a clear path to the major supply zone at 112.05-113.35. Watch the candles there closely – we don’t want to see a flame out like we saw in copper at 4.8310-4.8595! (Check Tuesday’s Evening Wrap for my recent copper analysis)

It’s all steady as she goes here with only a close below the short term trend ribbon / 104.20 likely to derail the gradual but resilient grind higher.

FTSE China A50 Index Futures

Better than most expected (click here for full size image)

We also covered the China A50 share index in ChartWatch in the Evening Wrap on 13 February.

Just a quick one here, because there's not a great deal to update apart from the fact the fledgling rally we identified just over a week ago remains intact.

That last candle is today's candle and is live – and given it's so far showing a nice demand-side white body and high close – if it can stay that way it will go a long way to locking in this new short term uptrend.

A short term uptrend that is developing nicely above the recently proven dynamic demand of the long term uptrend ribbon, with rising peaks and rising troughs, and with a growing predominance of demand-side candles.

Enough said, I don't want to jinx it! 🤞

data.title: Shanxi Zhendong Pharmaceutical's New TCM Drug Approved for Clinical Trials
data.description:
Shanxi Zhendong Pharmaceutical's innovative traditional Chinese medicine (TCM), Xinglou Chengqi Granules, received clinical trial approval from China's National Medical Products Administration (NMPA), according to a Wednesday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The drug, classified as TCM 1.1, is intended for treating acute ischemic stroke.

data.title: South Korean Shares End the Week Flat
data.description:
The benchmark KOSPI closed nearly flat at 2,655 points on Friday, as gains in battery, auto, and steelmakers offset sharp declines in chip stocks.

Investor sentiment was also lifted as South Korea and the US held talks to ease uncertainty over planned tariffs, with the Trade Minister stressing efforts to address potential duties on steel, aluminum, and automobiles that could impact the export-driven economy.

Meanwhile, expectations for a 25-basis-point rate cut by the Bank of Korea on Tuesday to support the sluggish economy also helped limit losses.

Among key stocks, Hyundai Motor (1.5%), Posco Holdings (5%), and Samsung SDI (2%) were the top performers.

On the downside, chipmakers Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix dropped 0.3% and 1.2%, respectively.

On the economic data front, South Korea's business sentiment improved for the second straight month in February 2025, rising to 65 from 63 in January.

Over the week, the benchmark index increased 2.5%, marking its third weekly gain.

data.title: Danish Business Morale Highest in 8 Months
data.description:
The manufacturing confidence indicator in Denmark increased to 107.3 in February 2025, up from 105.3 in the previous month.

It marked the highest manufacturing confidence since June 2024, as production expectations (17 vs 15 in January) and employment (12 vs 9) improved, with the sub-indices of employment rising to their highest in four months.
